shape_decode: cmp=L, vp=W, vpc=W, naive=W -> cmplog BLOCKS; value_profile + value_profile_cmplog + naive RESOLVE. Mixed shape: each branch carries an i2s-ANTI claim (_h0: naive>cmplog, I2S hurts) AND a vp-PRO claim (_h1: vpc>cmplog, CMP_MAP gradient rescues). Per contract, each hypothesis is assigned by its named mechanism+direction, not by the shape label.
round_note: Loop-until-dry round 1 re-design. SUPERSET of evidence_test.r0.json: the 3 prior hypotheses are KEPT verbatim so the 8 CONFIRMED branches (7x i2s_anti_structural_byte_corruption, 1x vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue) re-validate unchanged. TWO NEW hypotheses added for the 3 UNLABELED branches (libpng/3974, harfbuzz/5977, harfbuzz/5998), both over the now-BUILT depth_reach / value_distance_reached tools.

hypotheses.label: I2S RandReplace overwrites the exact structural/category/literal bytes naive havoc assembles, depleting (not enriching) the target byte in cmplog's corpus relative to naive
hypotheses.membership_change_note: harfbuzz_5977_h0 REMOVED vs r0: 5977 is an UNLABELED branch -- its _h0 did NOT confirm the byte_corruption rule in arbitration (no clean fixed-offset depletion; the gate is structural-STATE accumulation, not a fixed-offset structural byte). It is reassigned to the new i2s_anti_structural_state_undermutation (depth_reach). The 3 remaining members (5844/5947/5992) are among the 7 CONFIRMED corruption branches and are kept verbatim.

params.note: harfbuzz on-disk corpus available; these branches expose a fixed-ish offset structural/category byte (Halant H bytes, VPre record bytes, intact tag substrings, the 64/a8 repeated-record stride) that I2S substitution displaces

hypotheses.prediction: If I2S is ACTIVELY CORRUPTING the structural byte, cmplog's corpus is byte-DEPLETED at the target offset vs naive => signed_target_enrich < 0 (anti). An I2S-IRRELEVANT gate (the no-preimage class below) would instead return no_gate_signature; a pro-I2S gate would return signed_target_enrich > 0. So a clearly NEGATIVE enrichment discriminates active corruption from mere irrelevance.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out the 'I2S simply has no traction here' (no_gate_signature) and 'I2S helps' (positive enrich) alternatives: only ACTIVE depletion of the structural byte in the cmplog corpus produces signed_target_enrich < -0.3.
hypotheses.g2_independence: operand_enrichment compares cmplog-vs-naive corpus byte frequency, never the vp/vpc resolve signal that defined the shape; independent of the label-source.

hypotheses.label: Gate has no input-side CMP preimage (category-table / state-machine classification, length threshold, or internally-derived overflow sentinel); I2S substitution finds no fixed-offset value to copy, so it misallocates budget while naive havoc explores freely

params.note: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature on structural/length gates and on gates with no fixed-offset value operand. libpng_3974_h0 is RETAINED here (the no-preimage path) but is now ALSO covered by the new value_distance_reached test below; whichever axis returns a clean confirming signal labels it.

hypotheses.prediction: These gates have NO fixed-offset input-byte value to substitute (category-table dispatch, syllable-length threshold start+3<=end, derived overflow sentinel 0x7fffffff with no input preimage). operand_enrichment therefore returns no_gate_signature. A genuine fixed-offset value gate (the corruption class above) would instead return a definite signed_target_enrich; so 'no_gate_signature' positively discriminates the no-preimage class from byte-corruption.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Distinguishes the no-input-preimage class (no fixed-offset operand => no_gate_signature) from the active-corruption class (a real target byte => a numeric signed enrich). If the tool DID find a target offset and return a number here, this hypothesis would be REFUTED for that branch.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Same corpus-byte-frequency measurement, independent of the vp/vpc resolve pattern.
hypotheses.membership_change_note: harfbuzz_5998_h0 REMOVED from this hypothesis vs r0 -- it is reassigned to the new i2s_anti_structural_state_undermutation hypothesis (depth_reach), because its 'no_gate_signature' outcome under operand_enrichment was NON-discriminating (rule did not hold in arbitration; the gate is structural-STATE-accumulation, not just operand-absent). The remaining members are operand-absent gates whose no_gate_signature outcome is the intended positive signal.

hypotheses.label: VP CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient gives graded partial-progress feedback toward the gate comparand, letting value_profile/vpc retain near-miss inputs that approach the target while cmplog's edge-only binary feedback stalls

params.note: BUILT tool value_distance_reached (descriptor_key seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch) replaces the r0 not-yet-built 'vp_gradient_climb'. Comparands: category/codepoint equality states (harfbuzz indic/myanmar/USE category gates, 0x0D12 MALAYALAM codepoint). vp_min_distance/cmp_min_distance are the per-tool metric names.

hypotheses.prediction: If VP's gradient is the rescue mechanism, value_profile's corpus REACHES strictly closer to the gate comparand than cmplog's (vp_min_distance < cmp_min_distance, winner_closer=true) while cmplog plateaus far from the target. If both reached equal distance, the rescue would NOT be gradient-driven (refuted). This discriminates a true graded-approach rescue from a coincidental throughput/structural win.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out 'value_profile resolves for a non-gradient reason' (e.g. raw throughput like naive): under that alternative vp and cmplog would reach equal value-distance and distance_gap ~ 0. A gradient rescue REQUIRES vp to approach measurably closer than cmplog.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Measures corpus value-distance to the comparand, NOT the vpc-resolves flag that defined the shape. The shape says vpc RESOLVES; this test asks WHETHER it got there by graded approach -- an independent question (G2 satisfied).
hypotheses.rule_change_note: KEEP hypothesis, but rule expressed over the BUILT value_distance_reached metric names (winner_closer/distance_gap) instead of r0's placeholder vp_min_value_distance/distance_lift, which named an unbuilt tool. Mechanism, members' VP-gradient story, and direction unchanged; this re-validates the 1 confirmed branch.

hypotheses.label: The gate is reached only by accumulated structural glyph-category state built across a multi-glyph buffer (USE-shaper syllable reorder / ZWNJ-plus-following-glyph sequence); cmplog's I2SRandReplace spends mutation budget overwriting bytes with logged CMP operands instead of growing/varying the glyph buffer, so its seeds drive the branch line LESS DEEP (less category state accumulated) than naive's broad havoc -- an I2S-anti effect measurable as reduced per-seed iteration depth at the branch, NOT as operand byte depletion (there is no fixed-offset operand).
hypotheses.novelty_basis: Genuinely different mechanism from the two prior i2s-anti hypotheses. byte_corruption asserts a measurable byte-frequency DEPLETION at a fixed offset; no_preimage asserts operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (operand absent). Both proved NON-discriminating for these branches (the arbiter scored their rules and none held). This hypothesis instead claims an ITERATION-DEPTH deficit: cmplog's seeds execute the structural branch shallower than naive's, regardless of any single byte. It is measured by a DIFFERENT built tool (depth_reach) over a DIFFERENT signal (per-seed branch execution count), so it is not a relaxed-threshold re-test.

params.note: depth_reach runs naive/vpc winner-RESOLVING vs cmplog loser-BLOCKING seeds through libafl-harfbuzz-cov and reads the per-seed-normalized execution count at the branch line. For these structural-assembly gates (USE syllable reorder at the is_halant_use line / ZWNJ-following-glyph loop-exit) the depth proxy = how many glyph-category iterations the branch sees per seed. harfbuzz HAS a cov image (data_realities: on-disk corpus + cov image available). Docker per-branch ~30-60s.

hypotheses.prediction: If I2S is undermutating the structural state, cmplog's blocking seeds drive the branch line SHALLOWER per seed than the resolving (naive/vpc) seeds: winner_depth > loser_depth, winner_deeper=true, depth_lift >= 1.3. If instead cmplog's seeds reached EQUAL branch depth (depth_lift ~ 1) the I2S-undermutation story is REFUTED -- cmplog would have built the same structural state and stalled for another reason. So a clear depth deficit positively confirms the structural-state-undermutation mechanism.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out 'cmplog reaches the same structural depth but loses for a value/throughput reason' -- under that alternative depth_lift ~ 1 and the rule fails. It also distinguishes this class from the operand-absent no_preimage class (which makes no depth claim) and from byte_corruption (which claims a fixed-offset byte deficit, not a depth deficit). Only an actual per-seed iteration-depth deficit at the structural branch confirms it.
hypotheses.g2_independence: depth_reach measures the per-seed EXECUTION COUNT at the branch line (a structural property of the seeds), not the cmp=L/naive=W resolve flags that defined the shape. The shape says naive resolves and cmplog blocks; this test asks WHY -- whether cmplog's seeds simply never accumulate the glyph-category state -- an independent question.

hypotheses.label: For the libpng cHRM redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR(0x7fffffff) gate, value_profile's CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient over the 4-byte redy operand lets vp/vpc corpora land bitwise CLOSER to the 0x7fffffff sentinel than cmplog's edge-only corpus -- the same gradient-rescue mechanism as vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue but on a CONCRETE 4-byte scalar operand (0x7fffffff) that value_distance_reached can climb, where the prior vp_pro test had no operand_literal to anchor on for this branch.
hypotheses.novelty_basis: Distinct from the kept vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue ONLY in operand anchoring: this branch's operand IS a concrete 4-byte literal (0x7fffffff), so value_distance_reached has a real target to measure Hamming-closure against. In r0 libpng_3974_h1 was bundled into a vp_pro hypothesis pointing at an unbuilt tool, and its arbitration did not hold. This is a fresh, falsifiable scalar-operand test, not a relaxed-threshold re-run. NOTE: libpng is owned by server sB (data_realities: lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty on sB) -- s4 cannot score it, but per the cross-server rule the test is designed valid for the OWNING server, which has the libpng on-disk corpus.

params.note: redy fixed-point at cHRM-data offset 12 (buf+12). value_distance_reached numeric/Hamming mode over the 4-byte operand. operand width 4 is above the width<=2 chance-alignment caveat, so the closure signal is trustworthy. Scored on server sB (owns libpng corpus).

hypotheses.prediction: If VP's gradient drives the redy bytes toward the sentinel, the vp/vpc resolving corpus reaches strictly smaller Hamming distance to 0x7fffffff than cmplog's blocking corpus: vp_min_distance < cmp_min_distance, winner_closer=true, distance_gap >= 0.15. If both reached equal distance, the win was throughput-driven (naive raw-havoc), not gradient-driven, and this VP-pro hypothesis is REFUTED for the branch.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out the competing _h0 story that the resolution is pure naive throughput stumbling on the sentinel with no gradient: under throughput-only, vp and cmplog reach the same min Hamming distance (distance_gap ~ 0). A confirmed VP-pro requires vp to approach measurably closer than cmplog. The two axes (i2s-anti _h0 vs vp-pro _h1) are independent and can resolve differently.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Measures Hamming distance of the corpus to the 0x7fffffff operand, NOT the vpc-resolves flag. Independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: For the harfbuzz structural-assembly gates with no scalar comparand (USE syllable reorder is_halant_use / ZWNJ-plus-following-glyph loop-exit), value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient rewards partial progress in the category-comparison chain, so the vpc resolving corpus accretes deeper structural glyph-category state and drives the branch line DEEPER per seed than cmplog's blocking corpus -- the gradient-rescue mechanism expressed as an iteration-DEPTH lift, because there is no fixed scalar operand for value_distance_reached to climb.
hypotheses.novelty_basis: The kept vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue measures Hamming-closure to a concrete category/codepoint comparand via value_distance_reached; that tool has NOTHING to measure for 5977/5998 (no operand_literal, structural-state gate). This hypothesis carries the SAME VP-gradient story but measures it with the DIFFERENT built tool depth_reach over the per-seed branch-execution-DEPTH signal -- a falsifiable, distinct measurement, not a relaxed-threshold re-run of value_distance_reached.

params.note: depth_reach runs vpc winner-RESOLVING vs cmplog loser-BLOCKING seeds through libafl-harfbuzz-cov, per-seed-normalized branch-line execution count = how deep the structural glyph-category iteration goes. harfbuzz has the cov image. Same tool/run as i2s_anti_structural_state_undermutation but interrogating the vpc-vs-cmplog contrast (VP-pro) rather than naive-vs-cmplog.

hypotheses.prediction: If VP's gradient accretes the structural state, vpc's resolving seeds drive the branch line DEEPER per seed than cmplog's blocking seeds: winner_depth > loser_depth, winner_deeper=true, depth_lift >= 1.3. If both reach equal depth (depth_lift ~ 1), the vpc win is not gradient-driven depth-accretion and this VP-pro hypothesis is REFUTED for the branch.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out 'vpc reaches the same structural depth as cmplog but resolves for a non-gradient reason': under that alternative depth_lift ~ 1 and the rule fails. A confirmed VP-pro structural climb REQUIRES vpc to drive the branch measurably deeper than cmplog -- the gradient is doing depth-accretion work that cmplog's edge-only feedback cannot.
hypotheses.g2_independence: depth_reach measures the per-seed branch-line execution count (a structural property of the seeds), not the vpc-RESOLVES flag that defined the shape. The shape says vpc resolves and cmplog blocks; this asks WHETHER vpc's seeds carry deeper accumulated category state -- an independent question. (Caveat noted in decision_order_rationale: vpc-resolving seeds inherently sit at-or-past the gate, so the discriminating power comes from cmplog's blocking seeds being SHALLOWER, not merely from vpc being at the gate.)

decision_order_rationale: Per branch, the _h0 (i2s-anti) and _h1 (vp-pro) axes are INDEPENDENT and BOTH may confirm. i2s-anti axis order: (1) byte_corruption (definite negative enrich at a fixed offset); (2) structural_state_undermutation (per-seed depth deficit, for the structural-assembly gates that have no fixed offset); (3) no_preimage (no_gate_signature, the operand-absent residue). vp-pro axis: (4) cmpmap_gradient_rescue (category/codepoint comparands via value_distance_reached); (5) sentinel_value_climb (the libpng 0x7fffffff scalar via value_distance_reached). A branch can be evidence-confirmed on one axis, both, or neither; unconfirmed-on-both falls to fallback=inconclusive.
data_realities_respected: I2S is untagged in mutation_op: NO seed_lineage 'I2S event' descriptor proposed. operand_enrichment uses corpus BYTE FREQUENCY (depletion); the NEW i2s_anti_structural_state_undermutation uses per-seed EXECUTION-COUNT depth via depth_reach -- neither is a lineage event.
value_profile is a FEEDBACK not a mutator: both vp-pro hypotheses measure what the corpus REACHES (value_distance_reached Hamming-closure to the operand), never a lineage gradient event.

On-disk corpora + cov images: harfbuzz is on s4 (corpus + libafl-harfbuzz-cov image) -> all 10 harfbuzz branches scorable, INCLUDING the new depth_reach test (needs the cov image). libpng is on sB (lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty) -> libpng_3974 (_h0 operand_enrichment + _h1 vp_pro_sentinel_value_climb) is scored by sB which owns the corpus; s4 reports it unscored and the merge takes sB's verdict. Per the cross-server rule, libpng is NOT marked decidable:false merely because absent on s4.

Structural-assembly gates have different-sized W/L seeds so byte-alignment can fail: the structural-state-undermutation class explicitly uses depth_reach (per-seed execution count), NOT byte_freq, exactly because these gates have no fixed offset and mismatched seed sizes. depth_reach normalizes per seed.

depth_reach scope_limit honored: it is the ITERATION-DEPTH proxy (per-seed branch execution count), valid here because both structural gates (USE syllable reorder / ZWNJ-following loop-exit) are loop/iteration gates where deeper structural state = more branch-line executions. It is NOT used for the ctx context-reach subtype (out of scope).

decidable_false.rationale: No sub-group is judged honestly non-discriminable in this round. The 3 unlabeled branches all admit a built-tool discriminating test: harfbuzz_5977 and harfbuzz_5998 are loop/iteration structural-assembly gates whose I2S-anti deficit and VP-pro climb are both measurable as per-seed branch DEPTH (depth_reach, BUILT); libpng_3974 has a concrete 4-byte scalar operand (0x7fffffff) measurable by value_distance_reached (BUILT) on its owning server sB. If, after building, depth_reach returns equal depth for 5977/5998 (depth_lift ~ 1) the i2s-anti hypothesis is honestly REFUTED for those branches and they fall to fallback=inconclusive -- the test is falsifiable, so 'no clean result' is a correct outcome rather than a forced label.

notes.priors_kept: All 3 r0 hypotheses KEPT (superset): i2s_anti_structural_byte_corruption (verbatim rule), i2s_anti_no_input_preimage_misdirection (rule verbatim; one member harfbuzz_5998_h0 reassigned out to the new depth hypothesis because its no_gate_signature outcome was non-discriminating), vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue (mechanism/members/direction unchanged; rule re-expressed over the now-BUILT value_distance_reached metric names instead of r0's unbuilt-tool placeholders). The 8 confirmed branches re-validate.
notes.new_mechanisms: TWO new hypotheses for the 3 unlabeled branches. (1) i2s_anti_structural_state_undermutation [depth_reach] for harfbuzz_5977_h0 + harfbuzz_5998_h0: I2S undermutates accumulated glyph-category state, measured as a per-seed branch-DEPTH deficit -- a different signal/tool than byte-freq depletion or operand-absence. (2) vp_pro_sentinel_value_climb [value_distance_reached] for libpng_3974_h1: VP gradient Hamming-closes the 4-byte 0x7fffffff redy sentinel, anchored on the concrete operand the r0 vp_pro test lacked for this branch.
notes.why_priors_failed_for_the_3: harfbuzz_5977/5998 are STRUCTURAL-STATE-ACCUMULATION gates (USE syllable reorder, ZWNJ-following loop-exit) with NO fixed-offset operand and NO scalar comparand -- operand_enrichment yields a non-discriminating no_gate_signature and value_distance_reached has nothing to climb. The discriminating signal is iteration DEPTH (depth_reach). libpng_3974 DOES have a scalar operand (0x7fffffff) but r0 routed its _h1 through an unbuilt tool; the BUILT value_distance_reached resolves it.
notes.tools_used: All hypotheses now reference BUILT registry tools: operand_enrichment (2 i2s-anti hyps), depth_reach (1 new i2s-anti hyp), value_distance_reached (2 vp-pro hyps). NO unbuilt tool remains (r0's vp_gradient_climb placeholder is retired in favor of the built value_distance_reached).
notes.targets: 10/11 branches harfbuzz (s4: corpus + cov image), 1 libpng (sB: corpus). Cross-server merge takes each owning server's verdict.
